behavior_summary: This script implements a throwable/usable object called the 'death disk' with simple inspect text, a use action that places/launches the disk into the world as a projectile, and on-hit reactions that can retaliate against attackers.
U8P_DEATHDIS::look is the 'U8P_DEATHDIS::look' handler: it makes the item produce the short description string "death disk " via Item::bark.

U8P_DEATHDIS::use resolves the process' target, computes a direction and placement offset, verifies placement with canExistAt, spawns helper/visual methods, pushes and configures the world object, fires the disk via Item::shoot, and clears the per-item Q flag (setQ(0)).
U8P_DEATHDIS::0281 is a small spawned helper that queries the owner's facing direction (Npc::getDir) and spawns directional visual/effect METHOD::15E7 calls (twice with different args).

U8P_DEATHDIS::gotHit and U8P_DEATHDIS::hit check a per-item Q flag (Item::getQ). If Q is nonzero they set Q to 1 and, when an attacker is present and is a live NPC, spawn METHOD::0676 (likely a retaliatory effect) targeting the attacker.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DEATHDIS::look: single-entry path that calls Item::bark and returns (high confidence).

U8P_DEATHDIS::use: resolve process target -> read item/target coords (Item::getX/Y/Z) -> compute dir/offset (Item::getDirToCoords) -> per-direction adjustments -> canExistAt check -> if allowed spawn visual helpers and U8P_DEATHDIS::0281, Item::push to world, spawn cleanup FREE routines, Item::orStatus to set item flags, call Item::shoot to launch projectile, and finally Item::setQ(0) (medium-high confidence).
U8P_DEATHDIS::0281: query owner direction (Npc::getDir) and spawn METHOD::15E7 twice using the direction to select parameters (medium confidence).

U8P_DEATHDIS::gotHit / U8P_DEATHDIS::hit: read Item::getQ and early-return if zero; otherwise Item::setQ(1) and conditionally spawn METHOD::0676 when an attacker param exists, Item::isNpc returns true and Npc::isDead returns false (medium-high confidence).
Multiple spawn calls create asynchronous visual/effect and cleanup processes; Item::push/pop wrap lifecycle transitions for the placed world object (high confidence).
Loop/frame progression inside U8P_DEATHDIS::use; treated as control-flow, not explicit internal call.
open_questions: What exact gameplay semantics does the per-item Q field represent (armed, active, already-exploded, or cooldown)? (inferred, medium confidence).
What are the precise parameter meanings and gameplay effects of the spawned METHOD::0676 and METHOD::15E7 routines (visuals vs damage vs status effects)? (inferred, low-medium confidence).
Which exact status bits are set by Item::orStatus in use(), and how do those bits affect subsequent engine/AI behavior? (inferred, medium confidence).
Are the numeric offsets/flags passed into canExistAt and Item::shoot tuned for special collision/height behavior (e.g., ignoring actors, area effects)? (uncertain, low confidence).

main_function.name: U8P_DEATHDIS::look
main_function.purpose: Present the item's description when examined by a player/actor; produces the short string 'death disk ' via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_DEATHDIS::look) triggered when an actor examines/looks at the item.

function_map.name: U8P_DEATHDIS::look
function_map.role: Item 'U8P_DEATHDIS::look' handler; emits description string via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_DEATHDIS::use
function_map.role: Handle using/activating the death disk: resolve target, compute placement/direction, validate placement, spawn helpers, place and shoot the disk, clear per-item Q.

function_map.name: U8P_DEATHDIS::0281
function_map.role: Small spawned routine that reads NPC facing and spawns directional visual/effect METHOD::15E7 calls.

function_map.name: U8P_DEATHDIS::gotHit
function_map.role: Respond to damage: gate on per-item Q, set Q to 1, and possibly spawn METHOD::0676 to affect the attacker.

function_map.name: U8P_DEATHDIS::hit
function_map.role: Same as gotHit: on-hit reaction using per-item Q and optional spawning of METHOD::0676 against attacker.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn a directional visual/effect helper tied to the owner's facing direction after placement/shoot (inferred from spawn usage).
Behavior overlap: both implement the on-hit reaction pattern and call the same external retaliatory METHOD::0676 logic (independent routines but mirrored behavior).

external_calls.purpose: Display/vocalize the item's short description string to the inspecting process/player.
Resolve the intended use/aim target for the invoking process so coordinates/target item can be read.
Read source or item X coordinate as part of placement/shoot computation.
Read source or item Y coordinate as part of placement/shoot computation.
Read source or item Z coordinate (height) used in placement/shooting calculations.
Compute direction/WorldPoint toward the resolved target coordinates to orient the disk projectile and drive per-direction offsets.
Collision/terrain check to verify if the disk can be placed/launched from the computed coordinates and orientation.
Start asynchronous visual/effect and FREE cleanup routines tied to the placed/shooting disk.
Place the disk item into the world (convert inventory/held item to a world object instance) before shooting.
Restore or adjust item stack/placement parameters after push/spawn operations (used around lifecycle transitions).
Set status bits on the placed item (marking state like 'in-world' or other engine flags).
Emit/fire the disk as a projectile toward the computed WorldPoint with given parameters.
Clear or reset the per-item Q state at end of use (observed as setQ(0)).
Read the per-item Q flag to decide whether to respond to the hit.
Set the per-item Q flag to 1 when reacting to a hit (marks changed state).
Check if the attacker parameter references an NPC before further checks or retaliation.
Ensure the attacker NPC is alive before spawning the retaliatory METHOD::0676.
Spawn METHOD::0676 as a retaliation/effect targeted at the attacker when conditions are met.
Query owner/NPC facing direction to choose directional visual parameters.
Spawn METHOD::15E7 twice with arguments derived from the owner's facing direction to produce directional visual/effects.
Spawn cleanup/free helper routines to manage lifetime of the pushed world object (FREE::1C19, FREE::2A12 observed).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a throwable/usable object called the 'death disk' with simple inspect text, a use action that places/launches the disk into the world as a projectile, and on-hit reactions that can retaliate against attackers.
